  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c-ai-readiness", "name": "AI readiness", "aliases": ["AI readiness index"], "description": "An organization's overall preparedness to adopt and deploy AI, assessed across the pillars of strategy, infrastructure, data, governance, talent, and culture.", "grounding_span": "AI readiness", "location": "Strategy"},

    {"id": "c-external-data-quality", "name": "External data quality", "aliases": [], "description": "Quality checks and reliability processes for external data used for AI training.", "grounding_span": "external data used for AI training", "location": "Data"},

    {"id": "cl-strategy", "claim_text": "Strategy forms the backbone of AI readiness, emphasizing a well-defined approach, clear ownership, and sustainable financial planning.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Strategy forms the backbone of AI readiness, emphasizing a well—", "location": "Strategy"},

  "instruments": [
    {"id": "instr-cisco-ai-readiness", "name": "Cisco AI Readiness Assessment", "owner": "Cisco", "year": 2026, "method": "Self-assessment questionnaire organized into six pillars (Strategy, Infrastructure, Data, Governance, Talent, Culture) with single-select ordinal maturity response options.", "grounding_span": "Cisco AI Readiness Assessment - Cisco", "location": "document"}
  ],
  "measures": [

    {"id": "m3", "text": "Do you have a process in place to measure the impact of the deployment of AI / AI-powered solutions?", "response_type": "single-select ordinal", "grounding_span": "Do you have a process in place to measure the impact", "location": "Strategy"},
    {"id": "m4", "text": "Has your company established a financial strategy to ensure sustainable funding for AI deployment initiatives?", "response_type": "single-select ordinal", "grounding_span": "Has your company established a nancial strategy to", "location": "Strategy"},
    {"id": "m5", "text": "How is your company prioritizing budget allocation between AI deployment and other technological initiatives?", "response_type": "single-select", "grounding_span": "How is your company prioritizing budget allocation", "location": "Strategy"},

    {"id": "m10", "text": "As your AI projects grow in complexity and data volume, how prepared is your network to adapt to these accordingly?", "response_type": "single-select ordinal", "grounding_span": "As your AI projects grow in complexity and data volume, how prepared is", "location": "Infrastructure"},
    {"id": "m11", "text": "How seamlessly is your network infrastructure integrated with your AI systems to facilitate efficient data flow and processing?", "response_type": "single-select ordinal", "grounding_span": "How seamlessly is your network infrastructure integrated with your AI", "location": "Infrastructure"},
    {"id": "m12", "text": "How would you assess your organization's awareness and understanding of cybersecurity threats specific to AI and machine learning systems?", "response_type": "single-select ordinal", "grounding_span": "How would you assess your organization's awareness and understanding", "location": "Infrastructure"},

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "Fully adaptable: can instantly accommodate any AI computational needs", "suggested_edge": "has_response_level", "note": "Each measure carries an ordered set of ordinal maturity-level response options (e.g., 'Fully adaptable' down to 'Not scalable at all'); the schema has no edge/node type to capture individual response options or their ordinal ranking."},
    {"grounding_span": "How receptive is your Board to embracing the changes brought about by", "suggested_edge": "measured_across_organizational_tier", "note": "The four receptiveness measures (m44-m47) decompose 'receptiveness to AI change' across distinct organizational tiers (Board, Leadership Team, Middle Management, Employees); the schema cannot express that these measures assess the same construct at different organizational strata."},
    {"grounding_span": "Cisco AI Readiness Assessment - Cisco", "suggested_edge": "organized_into_pillar", "note": "The instrument groups its measures under six named pillars (Strategy, Infrastructure, Data, Governance, Talent, Culture); the schema lacks an edge linking an Instrument's measures to the pillar/section that scores them."}
  ]
}
